entries.d: nine o'clock in the morning
entries.s: School starts at _____ sharp in the morning, so we must be in the classroom by then.

entries.d: twelve o'clock in the middle of the day
entries.s: We eat lunch at _____ when the clock shows exactly twelve o'clock in the middle of the day.

entries.d: nine o'clock in the evening
entries.s: My favourite late TV show is on at _____ in the evening after dinner when it is dark outside.

entries.d: twelve o'clock at night
entries.s: On New Year's Eve, we stay awake until _____ — 12 o'clock at night — and then shout 'Happy New Year!'

entries.d: the time when it is exactly nine
entries.s: The evening film starts at _____ exactly, so we must take our seats by then in the cinema.

entries.d: fifteen minutes after nine
entries.s: It is _____ — 15 minutes after 9 — so we are late for school which started at 9.

entries.d: thirty minutes after nine
entries.s: I finish my breakfast at _____ — that is 30 minutes after 9.

entries.d: fifteen minutes before ten
entries.s: It is _____ — 15 minutes before 10 — and the shop closes at ten, so we must hurry.

entries.d: to travel on a bicycle
entries.s: I like to _____ with two wheels and pedals in the park on Sundays.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with BIKE?

entries.d: to look at programmes on television
entries.s: After dinner, we _____ together as a family on the sofa in the living room.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with TV?

entries.d: to play a game where you kick a ball
entries.s: The boys _____ with a round black-and-white ball on the green field every day after school.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with FOOTBALL?

entries.d: to play games on a computer
entries.s: My older brother likes to _____ on his console with a handheld controller in his free time.

entries.d: to make music on a piano
entries.s: She can _____ beautifully because she practises on her black and white keys an hour every day.

entries.d: to sit on a horse and move
entries.s: My older sister goes to a countryside farm every Sunday to _____ in the riding school.

entries.d: to ride on a small wooden board with four wheels
entries.s: He likes to _____ on his wooden board with four wheels in the park with his friends.

entries.d: to make food in the kitchen
entries.s: My dad likes to _____ spaghetti pasta with tomato sauce for dinner on Wednesdays.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with COOK?

entries.d: to travel on a scooter
entries.s: She likes to _____ with two small wheels and one foot pushing off the ground, to the shops.

entries.d: to go down snow on skis
entries.s: In winter we go up to the snowy mountains to _____ down the slopes on two long thin boards.

entries.d: to ride a board on snow
entries.s: He learns to _____ down the mountain on one wide board with both feet strapped on during the winter holidays.

entries.d: to move on ice with special shoes
entries.s: We go to the ice rink with blades under our feet to _____ smoothly across the frozen surface every Saturday.

entries.d: happening every day
entries.s: Walking our pet dog is my _____ job — I do it every single day before school without fail.

entries.d: time when you can do what you want
entries.s: In my _____, when I don't have school or homework, I like to read books and draw pictures.

entries.d: asking what the clock shows
entries.s: _____ — It's half past three in the afternoon now.

entries.d: a polite way to get someone's attention
entries.s: _____ Where is the train station from here? I am a tourist.

entries.d: to move or do something fast
entries.s: We are very late for school and it starts in two minutes. We must _____ and run fast!

entries.d: a thing on the wall that shows the time
entries.s: I looked at the wall _____ with hands pointing to numbers and saw it was already noon.

entries.d: telling someone the morning time
entries.s: _____ in the morning and the maths lesson is starting right now at school.

entries.d: telling someone the evening time
entries.s: _____ in the evening and the young children are getting into their pyjamas ready for bed.

entries.d: asking what the clock shows now
entries.s: _____ I don't want to be late for the doctor's appointment at 4 o'clock!

entries.d: the time when you go to sleep
entries.s: It is nine o'clock at night, so it is _____ for the young children. They must brush teeth and sleep.

entries.d: a time to stop and rest
entries.s: Let's have a short _____ from studying and eat our sandwiches in the yard for fifteen minutes.

entries.d: moving your body to stay healthy
entries.s: Running around the park and swimming in the pool are good kinds of _____ for your body.

entries.d: to get into bed to sleep
entries.s: I always _____ at exactly nine o'clock on school nights so I get enough sleep for the next day.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with BED?

entries.d: to travel to school each day
entries.s: My little brother _____ by yellow bus every weekday morning to learn maths and English.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with SCHOOL?

entries.d: not inside a building
entries.s: The warm sun is shining and the birds are singing. Let's play ball games _____ in the garden!

entries.d: to learn about something
entries.s: I need to _____ my English vocabulary and grammar for the big test tomorrow morning.

entries.d: to make someone stop sleeping
entries.s: My mum has to _____ my little brother every morning at seven because he sleeps deeply.

entries.d: very good and surprising
entries.s: The fireworks in the sky last night were _____! The best show I have ever seen.

entries.d: to open the door when someone knocks
entries.s: Someone is knocking on the front door right now. Can you please _____?

entries.d: a small tree with many branches
entries.s: The small cat is hiding behind the big green leafy _____ in the garden. I can only see its tail.

entries.d: wishing someone a good time
entries.s: You are going to the zoo with your class? _____ I hope you see the lions and elephants!

entries.d: to go where nobody can see you
entries.s: Let's play a game. Close your eyes for thirty seconds and I will _____ behind the big tree where you cannot see me.

entries.d: the sound when someone hits a door
entries.s: I heard a loud _____ — tap tap tap — at the front door. Someone was banging their fist.

entries.d: something you did not expect
entries.s: Close your eyes and don't peek — I have a wonderful _____ present hidden behind my back for you!

entries.d: to move something away from you
entries.s: Please _____ the heavy door with both hands to open it — it won't move the other way.

entries.d: a short message you send from a phone
entries.s: I got a short _____ on my phone from my friend about the birthday party this Saturday.

entries.d: to take care of someone or something
entries.s: Can you _____ my pet dog — feed him and walk him — while I am on holiday for one week?

entries.d: a way for cars and people to travel on
entries.s: Look carefully to the left and then the right before you cross the busy _____ with cars.

entries.d: a home or a location
entries.s: Come to my _____ — my house — after school today. We can play video games in my bedroom.

entries.d: a show on TV or radio
entries.s: There is a funny nature _____ on TV tonight at 7 o'clock about African animals.

entries.d: a hint that helps you find an answer
entries.s: I cannot guess the answer to the riddle. Please give me a _____ — a small hint — to help me.

entries.d: saying you will meet again quickly
entries.s: I have to go now to catch my bus home. _____! I will call you tomorrow!

entries.d: when white ice falls from the sky
entries.s: Look outside the window! It is _____ and the garden is slowly turning white with soft flakes.

entries.d: if it is sunny, rainy, cold, or warm
entries.s: The _____ is very nice and sunny today, so let's go to the park for a picnic.

entries.d: thirty minutes
entries.s: The next bus comes in exactly _____ — thirty minutes from now — so we must wait.

entries.d: telling someone to be faster
entries.s: _____! The train leaves the station in five minutes and we are still walking!
